articles.title: Gaming Commission Chair Maynard goes 'On the Record' about sports betting, prediction markets
Magic vs Pistons Win Probability for Game 1 at Prediction Markets
Polymarket wants to be 'news 2.0.' Is this a safe bet? | Opinion
articles.summary: Polymarket is positioning itself as the next evolution of news media, betting that prediction markets can deliver more accurate information than traditional journalism. If they pull it off, every major news outlet will either need to integrate prediction markets or risk looking like they're stuck in the stone age of punditry and hot takes.

articles.title: FanDuel wants to carve a sports niche in the controversial prediction market business
articles.summary: FanDuel is pushing into prediction markets beyond traditional sports betting, targeting political and current events wagering that's sparked regulatory battles across the US. The move could legitimize prediction markets for mainstream bettors while setting up a collision course with existing platforms like Kalshi and PredictIt over market share and regulatory turf.

articles.title: Prediction Markets are a $1 trillion market by 2030, Bernstein says
Philip Bump: Are prediction markets crowdsourced wisdom — or just prop bets?
articles.summary: Philip Bump questions whether prediction markets actually aggregate collective intelligence or function more like glorified sports betting on political outcomes. The framing matters because mainstream media coverage increasingly shapes how regulators and the public view the legitimacy of prediction markets as forecasting tools versus gambling platforms.

articles.title: NBA in discussions to partner with Kalshi, Polymarket
articles.summary: The NBA is exploring partnerships with prediction markets platforms Kalshi and Polymarket to potentially offer betting on basketball outcomes. This signals major sports leagues are finally embracing prediction markets as legitimate financial instruments, not just gambling sideshows, which could unlock massive mainstream adoption and liquidity.

articles.title: Betting on the news raises ethical questions for journalists
articles.summary: Journalists are placing bets on news events they cover, creating potential conflicts of interest between reporting objectively and having financial stakes in outcomes. This threatens the credibility of news sources that prediction markets rely on for accurate information, potentially corrupting the very foundation that makes these markets work.

articles.title: Do prediction markets make money from other peoples’ misery? | Fiona Katauskas
articles.summary: Someone's questioning whether prediction markets profit off human suffering and tragic events. The critique matters because public perception of markets betting on disasters or deaths could trigger regulatory crackdowns that hurt the entire industry.

articles.title: Can the S&P 500 sustain momentum? What Polymarket, analysts say
Wanna bet? Washington steps up scrutiny of prediction markets
articles.summary: Washington regulators are ramping up their oversight of prediction markets as the industry gains mainstream traction. This signals either legitimizing regulation that could unlock institutional money, or restrictive crackdowns that could kneecap the fastest-growing corner of the betting world.
